comments.text: No doubt this question already appears in the literature somewhere (do let me know if it does), but I suspect one could propose a more general mutual independence conjecture: let $0 \leq k_1 < \cdots < k_t$ be integers, and let $\alpha_1, \cdots, \alpha_t \in (0, 1)$. Must the density of $n$ satisfying $P(n + k_i) < (n + k_i)^{\alpha_i}$ for all $i$ exist and be equal to the product of all $\rho(1/\alpha_i)$? If this is true, it would resolve [369], and with a bit of analysis, I suspect it could also be used to resolve [372].

In theorem 1.14 from this paper, Teravainen proves this result in the logarithmically averaged case, as well as some other problems concerning correlations of the largest prime factor function. It follows mostly from Matomäki-Radziwill-Tao's results on multiplicative functions and Tao's entropy decrement argument (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
